senses.definition: someone who manages a theater company and also acts in their plays

examples.x: As an actor-manager, he not only starred in productions but also handled administrative duties and managed the theater company.
The tradition of the actor-manager dates back to the 18th century when performers took on managerial roles in addition to their acting responsibilities.
She thrived in her role as an actor-manager, balancing the demands of performance with the challenges of running a theater.
Actor-managers played a pivotal role in shaping the theater industry, influencing repertoire choices, casting decisions, and production styles.
He faced the pressures of multitasking as an actor-manager, often juggling rehearsals, performances, and business meetings.

senses.definition: someone who is in charge of the artistic features, such as props and costumes of a movie or play

examples.x: The art director oversees the visual elements of a film, ensuring consistency and creativity in every scene.
She was promoted to art director after demonstrating exceptional talent and leadership skills in previous projects.
The art director collaborated closely with the production designer to bring the director's vision to life on screen.
As the art director of the advertising agency, he leads a team of designers and illustrators to create captivating campaigns for clients.
The art director is responsible for setting the overall aesthetic tone and style of a magazine, website, or multimedia project.

senses.definition: someone whose job is to help an actor get dressed for a play or is in charge of their costumes

examples.x: The dresser assists actors backstage, ensuring they are properly attired for each scene of the play.
She worked as a dresser for the theater company, meticulously organizing costumes and accessories for the cast.
The dresser's role is crucial in ensuring quick costume changes during the fast-paced production.
He started as a dresser before eventually becoming a costume designer, inspired by the magic of theatrical transformations.
The dresser anticipates the needs of each actor, preparing their costumes and props in advance of the performance.

senses.definition: an actor who plays the main role in a play or movie

examples.x: The lead captivated audiences with his powerful performance in the title role of the play.
She auditioned for the lead in the school musical and was thrilled to be cast as the lead singer.
The director praised the lead for her dedication and talent in bringing the protagonist to life on screen.
He dreamed of becoming a lead in a Hollywood blockbuster and spent years honing his craft as an actor.
The lead's charisma and stage presence commanded attention whenever he stepped onto the stage.

senses.definition: someone who, on a regular basis, goes to the cinema

examples.x: The moviegoer eagerly awaited the release of the latest blockbuster film.
She considers herself a dedicated moviegoer, often spending weekends at the cinema catching up on new releases.
Moviegoers lined up outside the theater, eager to secure tickets for the highly anticipated premiere.
The moviegoer's enthusiasm for cinema led her to pursue a career in film studies.
Moviegoers were treated to a diverse selection of films at the international film festival.

senses.definition: the film industry of India, based in the city of Mumbai

examples.x: Bollywood is known for its vibrant dance sequences, melodious music, and larger-than-life storytelling.
She grew up watching Bollywood films and developed a deep appreciation for Indian cinema.
Bollywood movies often feature elaborate song and dance numbers that contribute to their unique charm.
The success of Bollywood films has led to increased international recognition and global audiences.
The actor rose to fame in Bollywood after starring in several blockbuster hits.

senses.definition: a well-known street in New York City where many theaters are located, which is considered the center of theater industry in the US

examples.x: Broadway is synonymous with the pinnacle of theatrical excellence, attracting audiences from around the world to its renowned theaters.
She fulfilled her lifelong dream of performing on Broadway, landing a leading role in a Tony Award-winning musical.
The bright lights of Broadway illuminate the iconic theater district, showcasing a diverse array of stage productions.
Broadway productions range from classic musicals to cutting-edge plays, catering to a wide range of audience preferences.
The Broadway musical captivated audiences with its unforgettable songs and dazzling choreography.

senses.definition: a movie, TV program, etc. that is based on a book or play

examples.x: The film adaptation of the bestselling novel stayed true to the book's plot and characters.
The adaptation of the classic Shakespearean play received critical acclaim for its innovative approach to the source material.
Hollywood has produced countless adaptations of literary works, bringing beloved stories to life on the silver screen.
The adaptation remained faithful to the original stage play, preserving its timeless themes and dialogue.
The adaptation of the Broadway musical featured elaborate sets and stunning choreography that dazzled audiences.

senses.definition: a piece of painted cloth that is hung at the back of a theater stage as part of the scenery

examples.x: The backdrop depicted a serene countryside scene, setting the mood for the pastoral play.
The stage crew worked tirelessly to hoist the massive backdrop into place for the opening night performance.
She painted the backdrop by hand, adding intricate details to create the illusion of a bustling cityscape.
The backdrop transformed seamlessly between scenes, transporting the audience to different locations within the story.
The backdrop added depth and dimension to the stage, enhancing the overall visual impact of the production.

senses.definition: the style in which a movie is shot

examples.x: The camerawork in the film was exceptional, capturing the action with precision and creativity.
The director collaborated closely with the cinematographer to ensure that the camerawork conveyed the desired mood and atmosphere.
The camerawork added depth and immersion to the virtual reality experience, transporting viewers into the heart of the action.
Viewers praised the camerawork for its seamless integration of handheld shots and aerial footage, enhancing the storytelling.

senses.definition: the process of assigning roles and parts to actors or performers in a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: The casting for the new film involved auditions with hundreds of actors vying for roles.
The director's vision for the film was realized through meticulous casting, with actors selected to embody specific characters.

senses.definition: the script and written instructions used in producing a motion picture

examples.x: She spent months crafting the screenplay for her debut feature film.
The screenplay won numerous awards for its compelling dialogue and intricate plot twists.
The screenwriter drew inspiration from real-life events to create a gripping screenplay that resonated with audiences.
He adapted the bestselling novel into a screenplay, staying true to the source material while enhancing its cinematic potential.
The screenplay underwent several revisions before being greenlit for production by the studio.

senses.definition: the most significant moment in a story, play, movie, etc. with a high dramatic suspense

examples.x: The climax of the novel left readers on the edge of their seats, eagerly anticipating the resolution.
She carefully crafted the climax of the play to elicit maximum emotional impact from the audience.
The film's climax featured a thrilling showdown between the hero and the antagonist, culminating in a dramatic confrontation.
The climax of the movie revealed the protagonist's true identity, unraveling a series of shocking revelations.
The climax of the play marked a turning point in the protagonist's journey, leading to a profound transformation.

senses.definition: a detailed and tightly framed photograph or film shot of a subject at close range

examples.x: The closeup of the actor's face conveyed a range of emotions, capturing the intensity of the moment.
She framed the shot as a closeup to highlight the protagonist's subtle expressions and inner turmoil.
The director opted for a closeup of the object to emphasize its significance within the scene.
The closeup of the character's hands added intimacy and authenticity to the portrayal of their actions.
Viewers were captivated by the closeup of the actress's eyes, which revealed a depth of emotion beyond words.

senses.definition: to stop filming or recording
to turn a book, story, or an event into a movie or play

examples.x: Historical accounts of great leaders are often dramatized in biographical films to engage modern audiences.
The playwright decided to dramatize the novel, adapting it into an engaging and visually stunning stage production.
The acclaimed director was hired to dramatize the bestselling novel into a blockbuster film.
The playwright took creative liberties to dramatize the historical event, bringing it to life on stage.
The producers decided to dramatize the true crime story for television, capturing the public's attention with its gripping narrative.

senses.definition: to change the original language of a movie or TV show into another language

examples.x: The studio hired professional voice actors to dub the foreign film into English for international audiences.
The television network plans to dub the popular Korean drama into Spanish to expand its audience base.
She was hired to dub the lead character in the Japanese anime series for its English release.
The film distributor decided to dub the classic French movie into Mandarin to appeal to the Chinese market.
The movie studio opted to dub the dialogue rather than use subtitles for the theatrical release.

senses.definition: to explain the events taking place in a movie, documentary, etc. as part of the program itself

examples.x: The documentary was narrated by a renowned actor, whose voice added gravitas to the storytelling.
She was chosen to narrate the wildlife documentary, drawing on her background in environmental science to provide insightful commentary.
He narrated the sports documentary with enthusiasm, bringing the thrilling moments of the game to life through his commentary.
The audiobook was narrated by the author, allowing listeners to experience the story in the way it was intended to be told.
She was asked to narrate the historical reenactment, guiding audiences through key moments in the past with her captivating storytelling.

senses.definition: to play the role of a character in a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: She portrayed the protagonist in the critically acclaimed film, earning praise for her nuanced performance.
He was chosen to portray the iconic historical figure in the epic biopic, showcasing his versatility as an actor.
The actor's ability to portray villains with depth and charisma has made him a sought-after talent in Hollywood.
She worked closely with the director to accurately portray the mannerisms and speech patterns of the real-life person she was portraying.

senses.definition: a long movie full of adventure that could be about a historical event

examples.x: The director's latest film was hailed as an epic, spanning generations and depicting the struggles of a nation.
She was captivated by the epic's vivid descriptions of battles and conquests, feeling as though she were part of the action.
The epic recounted the exploits of legendary figures, inspiring awe and wonder in its readers.

senses.definition: a full-length movie that has a story

examples.x: The director's latest project is a feature film that explores themes of love and loss in a post-apocalyptic world.
She attended the premiere of the feature film at the prestigious film festival, eager to see the culmination of years of hard work.
The studio invested millions of dollars into the production of the feature film, hoping to launch a successful franchise.
He landed a leading role in the feature film after impressing casting directors with his audition tape.
She wrote the screenplay for the feature film, drawing inspiration from her own life experiences.

senses.definition: one of a series of photographs forming a movie or video
a large lamp that produces a powerful beam of light used for lighting areas such as sports grounds or the outside of buildings

examples.x: The stadium was illuminated by powerful floodlights, casting a bright glow over the playing field.
The film set was bathed in the warm glow of floodlights, creating a cinematic ambiance for the shoot.
The concert stage was illuminated by an array of colorful floodlights, adding to the excitement of the performance.
The photographer used floodlights to create dramatic lighting effects in the studio, enhancing the mood of the photoshoot.
The tennis court was brightly lit with floodlights, allowing players to continue their match well into the evening.

senses.definition: a device used for making images or videos appear on a screen, wall, or other flat surfaces

examples.x: The classroom was equipped with a state-of-the-art projector, allowing teachers to display educational videos and presentations.
They set up a projector in the backyard for an outdoor movie night, casting larger-than-life images onto a white sheet hung between trees.
The conference room had a ceiling-mounted projector for business presentations, ensuring clear visibility for all attendees.
He brought his portable projector to the camping trip, turning the side of the tent into a makeshift movie theater under the stars.
The art installation used projectors to project images onto the walls of the gallery, creating an immersive visual experience for visitors.

senses.definition: a circular item around which film is wound

examples.x: The film technician carefully loaded the reel with the latest movie release, preparing it for screening at the cinema.
He rummaged through the attic, searching for an old reel of home movies from his childhood.
She wound the wire around the reel, securing it in place before connecting it to the electronic device.
The antique store featured an assortment of old-fashioned film reels, relics from the golden age of cinema.
The engineer examined the steel reel, checking for any signs of wear or damage before using it to spool the cable.

senses.definition: a representation that an actor or a performer gives of an artistic or musical piece that shows their understanding and feeling toward it

examples.x: His interpretation of the character brought new depth and complexity to the stage production, earning him critical acclaim.
The actor's interpretation of the Shakespearean soliloquy was riveting, drawing the audience into the character's inner turmoil.
The singer's interpretation of the ballad moved listeners to tears, evoking a sense of nostalgia and longing.
The dancer's interpretation of the choreography was both graceful and powerful, conveying a range of emotions through movement.
The comedian's interpretation of the classic joke had the audience roaring with laughter, demonstrating his comedic timing and wit.

senses.definition: the first public screening or performance of a movie or play

examples.x: The red carpet was rolled out for the premiere of the highly anticipated blockbuster movie.
Fans lined up outside the theater for the premiere of the latest superhero film, eager to be among the first to see it.
The director nervously awaited the audience's reaction at the premiere of her debut feature film.
The theater was packed for the premiere of the Broadway musical, with critics and theatergoers alike eager to witness the production.
Celebrities and industry insiders attended the star-studded premiere of the indie film, generating buzz and excitement for its release.

senses.definition: the music composed for a movie

examples.x: The composer received critical acclaim for his hauntingly beautiful score for the epic film.
The orchestra rehearsed tirelessly to perfect the intricate score for the upcoming ballet performance.
The director collaborated closely with the composer to ensure the score captured the mood and emotion of each scene.
The film's score won an Academy Award for Best Original Music, cementing its place in cinematic history.
The composer drew inspiration from the film's storyline to create a poignant and evocative score that resonated with audiences.

senses.definition: a lamp that produces a very strong beam of light that can be cast on someone or something, particularly a person on stage

examples.x: The actor stepped into the spotlight, his face illuminated by the warm glow of the stage spotlight.
The singer dazzled the audience as she moved gracefully under the spotlight, her voice soaring with each note.
The magician performed his tricks under the bright spotlight, captivating the audience with his sleight of hand.
The dancer twirled and leaped across the stage, her movements accentuated by the dramatic spotlight.
The speaker stood confidently in the spotlight, delivering a powerful speech that resonated with the audience.

senses.definition: the part of the theater where performers, crew, and staff work away from the audience's sight

examples.x: Backstage was filled with nervous energy before the curtain rose.
Backstage housed racks of elaborate outfits.
The backstage was buzzing with activity as the crew set up the stage.
Backstage fell silent as the opening note sounded in the auditorium.
The backstage was crowded with people preparing for the show.

senses.definition: very well-known and admired

examples.x: The actor gave a legendary performance that will be remembered for generations to come.
She became a legendary figure in the music industry, with fans worldwide singing her praises.
The chef's legendary culinary skills earned him a Michelin star and international acclaim.
The author penned a series of legendary novels that captivated readers with their rich characters and intricate plots.
The rock band gave a legendary concert, electrifying the crowd with their unforgettable performance.

senses.definition: related or belonging to the theater or acting

examples.x: She had a theatrical flair for storytelling, captivating her audience with dramatic gestures and expressive voice.
The actor's theatrical training shone through in his dynamic performance on stage, commanding the audience's attention from start to finish.
The director's vision for the production was bold and theatrical, incorporating elaborate sets and costumes to create a visually stunning experience.
The playwright's latest work was a masterpiece of theatrical innovation, pushing the boundaries of traditional storytelling.
Her gestures were theatrical, as if she were performing on a grand stage rather than simply conversing in a cafe.

senses.definition: used to wish a person good luck, particularly before their performance

examples.x: Jason sent Phillip a fax from the airport before Monday's show, with the greeting: `Break a leg and enjoy yourself.'
Before the play, the director said to the cast, 'Break a leg,' to wish them success.
She whispered to her friend backstage, "Break a leg out there; you'll do great!"
The band members gave each other a pep talk and said, 'Break a leg' before going on stage.

senses.definition: a large space at the entrance of a hotel or theater where people can wait or meet

examples.x: Guests mingled in the elegant foyer of the hotel, admiring the grand chandeliers and marble floors.
The theater's foyer buzzed with excitement as patrons gathered before the show, sipping cocktails and perusing the program.
She greeted her friends in the spacious foyer of the art gallery, marveling at the stunning exhibits on display.
The hotel's foyer was adorned with exquisite artwork and plush seating areas, creating a welcoming atmosphere for arriving guests.
The theater's foyer served as a bustling hub of activity, with ticket holders lining up at the box office and concession stands.
